scope.summary: F-LOW DK1 was already implemented in driver commit 1912455: an explicit atomic handle count elects exactly one final PoolEngine drop, preventing the observational Arc strong-count race from losing eager close.
out_of_scope: DK2's TIMEDWAIT-thread work, which happened to share the implementation commit but is tracked separately.
Live Oracle pool lifecycle testing; the landed regression test uses the driver's FakeBackend.
Any new driver source change: this close records verification of an already-landed commit.

entry_points.name: PoolEngine Clone/Drop (rust-oracledb)
entry_points.trace: The checksum-bound driver commit 1912455 increments a shared AtomicUsize in Clone and uses fetch_sub(Ordering::AcqRel) in Drop, so only the 1-to-0 transition calls close_all_connections. racing_pool_handle_drops_close_exactly_once creates 16 clones, holds all drop calls at a Barrier, joins every thread, waits for worker exit, then asserts exactly one backend close.

limitations: The current-session rerun was bounded to 180 seconds and timed out compiling asupersync in a fresh dedicated target. This close does not claim that test passed in this session.
The acceptance test itself is not a happy-path single-thread test: it has a concrete 16-thread Barrier interleaving and an exact-one close assertion, verified by committed-source inspection.
Cross-repo close: this checkout records a checksum-bound rust-oracledb commit; no driver source was changed for this verification.
